scope.summary: Propagate optional host-native tool inventory through hooks-mux AGENT_CAPABILITIES_JSON into SDK unified PromptContext and process creation prompt rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Host tool inventory is structured and optional/additive on the serialized adapter capability contract.
Existing adapters and SDK consumers continue to work when tool inventory is absent.
Claude adapter populates reliable host tool descriptors or partial descriptors without pretending to know unavailable metadata.
SDK unified ProxyCapabilities preserves the inventory from AGENT_CAPABILITIES_JSON.
PromptContext exposes host-native tools distinctly from coarse capabilities and external agent discovery.
Process creation prompt includes concise guidance such as host can do X and external agents should be used for Y when host inventory is present.
Focused no-model tests cover hooks-mux serialization, SDK env parsing, createUnifiedContext, prompt rendering, and absent-inventory fallback behavior.
qualityGates: Targeted Vitest suites for hooks-mux capability serialization and SDK unified prompt context.
Claude adapter test for populated host tool inventory.
Prompt composer test proving host tool section appears in process creation prompt and stays absent when no inventory exists.
riskControls: Keep schema optional to avoid breaking existing AGENT_CAPABILITIES_JSON payloads.
Use concise prompt rendering to avoid prompt bloat.
Keep host-native tools in a separate prompt section from external agent discovery.
Do not add AGENT_TOOLS_JSON unless contract design finds a concrete reason the existing JSON cannot safely carry the optional field.
